jsonLdBlocks.name: Woogoro Roofing Quote Analyzer
jsonLdBlocks.description: Upload any roofing contractor quote to check if the price is fair. AI-powered analysis with local pricing data.

jsonLdBlocks.name: Woogoro Roofing Quote Analyzer
jsonLdBlocks.description: Free analyzer for roofing quotes. Upload your quote and check the price, scope, and red flags. No email or signup required.

jsonLdBlocks.name: How to check if your roofing quote is fair
jsonLdBlocks.description: Use the Woogoro analyzer to compare a contractor quote against city-specific roofing pricing in under a minute.

tool.name: Your roofing contractor quote (PDF, photo, or text)

step.name: Upload your quote
step.text: Drag your roofing quote into the analyzer or paste the text. PDFs, photos, and screenshots all work.

step.name: Enter your city
step.text: Choose your city so pricing is benchmarked against local cost-of-living and labor rates.

step.name: Review the price check
step.text: See where your quote falls in the local price range, what scope items may be missing, and any red flags in the fine print.

step.name: Use the questions to push back
step.text: Take the contractor follow-up questions Woogoro generates into your conversation with the contractor.

mainEntity.name: What should be on a legitimate roofing quote?

acceptedAnswer.text: Total installed cost, roof size in squares, shingle brand + product line, underlayment type, ice and water shield coverage, flashing replacement scope, drip edge and starter strip, ridge vent specs, decking repair allowance, permit details, and warranty terms (manufacturer + workmanship).

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my roofing quote is a fair price?

acceptedAnswer.text: Compare the per-square or per-square-foot price against local benchmarks for the same shingle tier and scope. A typical architectural shingle job runs $4.50-$8.50/sq ft installed. Lower than that usually means skipped tear-off, cheap underlayment, or reused flashing. Higher than $10/sq ft should include premium shingles or complex pitch/access.

mainEntity.name: What are red flags in a roofing quote?

acceptedAnswer.text: Roof-over instead of tear-off, missing underlayment brand, no drip edge or starter strip listed, no decking repair rate, mixed-brand materials claiming a "lifetime" warranty, vague "standard" workmanship warranty, and missing permit details.

mainEntity.name: What's the difference between workmanship and manufacturer warranty?

acceptedAnswer.text: Manufacturer warranty covers defective shingles (25 years to lifetime from GAF, Owens Corning, etc.) but often excludes labor to replace them. Workmanship warranty (2-10 years from contractor) covers install errors, leaks, and labor. Both matter - a cheap contractor with a 1-year workmanship warranty is a risk.

mainEntity.name: Should I accept a verbal quote or emailed number?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Every legitimate roofing contract should be itemized in writing with scope, materials, warranty terms, timeline, payment schedule, and license/insurance details. Verbal agreements are effectively unenforceable when problems arise.
